prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(register_entity_for_netting source_subsidiary_a)
(register_entity_for_netting destination_subsidiary_b)
(assign_settlement_channel_to_entity source_subsidiary_a settlement_channel_rail_1)
(assign_settlement_channel_to_entity destination_subsidiary_b settlement_channel_rail_2)
(attach_transfer_request_to_entity source_subsidiary_a transfer_request_tx_1)
(approve_entity_for_processing source_subsidiary_a transfer_request_tx_1)
(assign_funding_account_to_entity source_subsidiary_a funding_account_operational)
(select_source_cash_account_for_matching source_subsidiary_a cash_account_source_pool_1 transfer_request_tx_1)
(bind_funding_account_to_source_entity source_subsidiary_a cash_account_source_pool_1 funding_account_operational)
(release_funding_account_from_entity source_subsidiary_a funding_account_operational)
(release_transfer_request_from_entity source_subsidiary_a transfer_request_tx_1)
(attach_transfer_request_to_entity destination_subsidiary_b transfer_request_tx_1)
(approve_entity_for_processing destination_subsidiary_b transfer_request_tx_1)
(assign_funding_account_to_entity destination_subsidiary_b funding_account_operational)
(select_destination_cash_account_for_matching destination_subsidiary_b cash_account_destination_pool_1 transfer_request_tx_1)
(bind_funding_account_to_destination_entity destination_subsidiary_b cash_account_destination_pool_1 funding_account_operational)
(release_funding_account_from_entity destination_subsidiary_b funding_account_operational)
(assemble_settlement_order_ready_source_selected_dest_selected source_subsidiary_a destination_subsidiary_b cash_account_source_pool_1 cash_account_destination_pool_1 settlement_order_001)
(release_transfer_request_from_entity destination_subsidiary_b transfer_request_tx_1)
(attach_transfer_request_to_entity source_subsidiary_a transfer_request_tx_1)
(mark_settlement_order_execution_ready settlement_order_001 source_subsidiary_a transfer_request_tx_1)
(authorize_settlement_execution_for_source_subsidiary source_subsidiary_a settlement_order_001)
(authorize_settlement_execution_for_destination_subsidiary destination_subsidiary_b settlement_order_001)
(allocate_funding_resource_to_entity source_subsidiary_a funding_resource_cash_t0 transfer_request_tx_1)
(finalize_settlement_for_source_subsidiary_and_release_resources source_subsidiary_a settlement_channel_rail_1 funding_resource_cash_t0)
(release_transfer_request_from_entity source_subsidiary_a transfer_request_tx_1)
(attach_transfer_request_to_entity destination_subsidiary_b transfer_request_tx_1)
(allocate_funding_resource_to_entity destination_subsidiary_b funding_resource_cash_t0 transfer_request_tx_1)
(finalize_settlement_for_destination_subsidiary_and_release_resources destination_subsidiary_b settlement_channel_rail_2 funding_resource_cash_t0)
(register_entity_for_netting netting_job_alpha)
(register_entity_for_netting netting_job_beta)
(assign_settlement_channel_to_entity netting_job_alpha settlement_channel_rail_3)
(attach_transfer_request_to_entity netting_job_alpha transfer_request_tx_1)
(approve_entity_for_processing netting_job_alpha transfer_request_tx_1)
(assign_funding_account_to_entity netting_job_alpha funding_account_operational)
(assign_liquidity_limit_to_netting_job netting_job_alpha liquidity_limit_default)
(assign_regulatory_flag_to_netting_job netting_job_alpha regulatory_flag_watchlist)
(attach_compliance_approval_to_netting_job netting_job_alpha compliance_approval_exemption_a)
(reserve_liquidity_buffer_for_settlement_order netting_job_alpha liquidity_buffer_pool_a settlement_order_001)
(confirm_liquidity_buffer_allocation_for_order netting_job_alpha liquidity_buffer_pool_a settlement_order_001 transfer_request_tx_1)
(apply_fee_and_prepare_job_for_execution_variant1 netting_job_alpha liquidity_limit_default funding_account_operational liquidity_buffer_pool_a settlement_order_001)
(init_regulatory_processing_for_netting_job netting_job_alpha regulatory_flag_watchlist liquidity_buffer_pool_a settlement_order_001)
(finalize_compliance_checks_and_enable_settlement netting_job_alpha)
(release_funding_account_from_entity netting_job_alpha funding_account_operational)
(release_liquidity_limit_from_netting_job netting_job_alpha liquidity_limit_default)
(release_regulatory_flag_from_netting_job netting_job_alpha regulatory_flag_watchlist)
(allocate_funding_resource_to_entity netting_job_alpha funding_resource_cash_t0 transfer_request_tx_1)
(finalize_settlement_for_netting_job_and_release_resources netting_job_alpha settlement_channel_rail_3 funding_resource_cash_t0)
(release_transfer_request_from_entity netting_job_alpha transfer_request_tx_1)
(assign_settlement_channel_to_entity netting_job_beta settlement_channel_rail_1)
(attach_transfer_request_to_entity netting_job_beta transfer_request_tx_1)
(approve_entity_for_processing netting_job_beta transfer_request_tx_1)
(assign_funding_account_to_entity netting_job_beta funding_account_operational)
(assign_liquidity_limit_to_netting_job netting_job_beta liquidity_limit_default)
(assign_regulatory_flag_to_netting_job netting_job_beta regulatory_flag_watchlist)
(attach_compliance_approval_to_netting_job netting_job_beta compliance_approval_exemption_b)
(reserve_liquidity_buffer_for_settlement_order netting_job_beta liquidity_buffer_pool_b settlement_order_001)
(confirm_liquidity_buffer_allocation_for_order netting_job_beta liquidity_buffer_pool_b settlement_order_001 transfer_request_tx_1)
(apply_fee_and_prepare_job_for_execution_variant1 netting_job_beta liquidity_limit_default funding_account_operational liquidity_buffer_pool_b settlement_order_001)
(init_regulatory_processing_for_netting_job netting_job_beta regulatory_flag_watchlist liquidity_buffer_pool_b settlement_order_001)
(finalize_compliance_checks_and_enable_settlement netting_job_beta)
(allocate_funding_resource_to_entity netting_job_beta funding_resource_cash_t0 transfer_request_tx_1)
(finalize_settlement_for_netting_job_and_release_resources netting_job_beta settlement_channel_rail_1 funding_resource_cash_t0)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a classical planning problem in PDDL for a treasury internal transfer netting domain. I need to output only the executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, one per line, nothing else.


## Understanding the Problem  
- The task is a classical planning problem in the treasury internal transfer netting domain using PDDL.  
- The output must be an executable plan, consisting of ground PDDL actions, listed one per line, with no additional content.

## Examining Entity States  
- No entities are registered or approved; registration is needed for source_subsidiary_a, destination_subsidiary_b, netting_job_alpha, and netting_job_beta.  
- Netting jobs (alpha and beta) are legal entities, inheriting from participant_root, alongside subsidiaries.  

## Analyzing Settlement Process  
- Settling entities requires finalize_settlement_for_..._and_release_resources, needing entity_funding_allocation_confirmed, entity_assigned_settlement_channel, and entity_allocated_funding_resource.  
- Entity funding allocation confirmed comes from allocate_funding_resource_to_entity, which requires settlement_authorized, entity_assigned_transfer_request, and funding_resource_available.  

## Investigating Action Paths  
- Settlement authorization can stem from actions like finalize_compliance_checks, finalize_job_confirmation, or authorize_settlement_execution for subsidiaries.  
- Netting jobs have additional actions like assign_liquidity_limit_to_netting, with liquidity buffers and priority directives already assigned.  

## Handling Transfer Requests  
- Only one transfer_request_tx_1 is available, limiting attachment to entities, as actions often require entity_assigned_transfer_request.  
- Subsidiaries may need the transfer request, while netting jobs might follow different paths, possibly not requiring it.  

## Planning Entity Registration  
- Register entities using register_entity_for_netting, which requires entities not registered and not settled, with 3 settlement channels available.  
- After registration, assign_settlement_channel_to_entity and possibly attach_transfer_request_to_entity, considering the single transfer request limitation.

## Exploring Settlement Steps  
- Processing subsidiaries requires preparing both source_subsidiary_a and destination_subsidiary_b for settlement, involving registration, channel assignment, and transfer request attachment.  
- Only one transfer_request_tx_1 is available, suggesting sequential processing where the transfer request is attached, used, and potentially released between entities.  

## Planning Entity Preparation  
- Register both subsidiaries first, then assign settlement channels, such as settlement_channel_rail_1 for source and settlement_channel_rail_2 for destination.  
- Attach transfer_request_tx_1 to source_subsidiary_a, approve it, and bind funding account, marking it ready for settlement, but dest needs similar steps.  

## Managing Funding Allocation  
- Funding resources, including funding_account_operational, need allocation to each entity, but only one resource is available, requiring sequential processing.  
- Actions like select_source_cash_account_for_matching and bind_funding_account_to_source_entity prepare entities for settlement, but coordination with destination is necessary.
Continuing from the existing summary, here’s a concise breakdown of the new reasoning content, ensuring flow and continuity:

## Managing Funding Allocation  
- Explored using offset paths like reserve_offset_instruction_for_source_entity, which doesn’t require a transfer request (tr) initially, marking the source for assembly without immediate tr assignment.  
- Applying offset instructions (apply_offset_instruction_for_source_entity) later requires assigned_tr, suggesting a potential alternative to bind funding for flexibility in sequencing.  

## Resolving Transfer Request Conflicts  
- Found sequence issues where preparing both source and destination (dest) for settlement requires assigned_tr at different stages, like select and allocate, leading to conflicts when releasing tr between entities.  
- Realized releasing tr from source after binding allows dest preparation, but later steps like mark_settlement_order_execution_ready still need assigned_tr for the source, creating a deadlock.  

## Exploring Sequence Adjustments  
- Considered preparing source without releasing tr until after assembly, but dest select still needs tr, complicating the process.  
- Determined that assembling both entities (assemble_ready_source_selected_dest_selected) can happen without tr preconditions, potentially resolving timing issues.

## Exploring Sequence Adjustments  
- Realized both source and destination can be prepared (ready, selected, marked assembly) by registering, assigning channels, and attaching/approving transfer requests (tr), with funding accounts (fa) bound and optionally released later.  
- Found assembling settlement order (assemble_settlement_order_ready_source_selected_dest_selected) works once both are ready, with no tr preconditions, allowing flexibility in execution.  

## Resolving Transfer Request Conflicts  
- Noted after assembly, marking execution ready (mark_settlement_order_execution_ready) requires re-attaching tr to source, as it was previously released, creating timing issues.  
- Identified that finalizing settlement for source (finalize_settlement_for_source_subsidiary_and_release_resources) doesn’t automatically release tr, requiring manual release post-settlement, which impacts dest allocation needing assigned tr.  

## Managing Resource Allocation  
- Confirmed allocating funding resource to source (allocate_funding_resource_to_entity) works once authorized, with tr assigned, confirming funding and freeing the resource for reuse.  
- Realized dest allocation similarly needs tr assigned, but sequence shows tr remains with source after settling, necessitating re-attachment to dest before proceeding.

## Managing Liquidity Buffer Allocation  
- Reserving liquidity buffer (reserve_liquidity_buffer_for_settlement_order) for netting job alpha ties it to settlement order 001, making the buffer unavailable for others.  
- Confirming liquidity buffer allocation (confirm_liquidity_buffer_allocation_for_order) requires no prioritized flag on the settlement order, which aligns with the current setup using assemble_ready_source_selected_dest_selected.  

## Handling Compliance and Fees  
- Compliance preconditions are met for netting job alpha after confirming buffer allocation, as transfer request is assigned and no prioritized flag exists.  
- Applying fees (apply_fee_and_prepare_job_for_execution_variant1) requires assigning a funding account to the netting job, which was previously available after releasing it from subsidiaries.  

## Preparing for Regulatory Processing  
- After fees are attached via variant1, the netting job is ready for regulatory processing, as settlement order has no fee or prioritized flags.  
- Initializing regulatory processing (init_regulatory_processing_for_netting_job) can proceed, given allocated liquidity buffer and no compliance or fee review needed yet.

## Extending Regulatory Processing  
- For netting job beta, initializing regulatory processing requires fees attached, confirmed after applying fees with funding account assigned.  
- Settlement order for beta has no prioritized or fee flags, aligning with the path for regulatory initialization used for alpha.  

## Managing Liquidity Buffer for Beta  
- Reserving liquidity buffer pool_b for beta sets it as assigned to the settlement order, distinct from alpha’s pool_a, with no conflicts noted.  
- Confirming liquidity buffer allocation for beta requires the buffer to be reserved, ensuring availability before proceeding.  

## Handling Resource Assignments  
- Liquidity limits and regulatory flags, previously assigned to alpha, can be released post-fee application and reassigned to beta as needed.  
- Funding accounts must be released from alpha after fee application and reassigned to beta, ensuring compliance with execution variant prerequisites.  

## Confirming Channel and Compliance  
- Netting jobs require entity channel assignment confirmed, achieved via assign_settlement_channel, ensuring readiness for finalization.  
- Compliance checks for beta show no priority evaluated flag set, matching the path that doesn’t require it, with compliance and fee reviewed conditions met.

## Continuing Resource Assignments  
- Transfer request tx_1 is attached and approved for netting job beta, ensuring processing readiness with funding resource cash_t0 allocated.  
- Liquidity limit default and regulatory flag watchlist are assigned to beta, with compliance approval exemption_b attached for processing.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
